questions.question: If ∆ABC ≅ ∆FED under the correspondence ABC ↔ FED, write all the corresponding congruent parts of the triangles.
questions.answer: Sides: AB=FE, BC=ED, AC=FD; Angles: ∠A=∠F, ∠B=∠E, ∠C=∠D

questions.question: State the condition under which two angles are said to be congruent.
questions.answer: Two angles are congruent if they have the same measure (magnitude).

questions.question: Can two triangles be congruent if all three corresponding angles are equal (AAA)? Justify.
questions.answer: No, AAA is not a congruence criterion; the triangles may have the same shape but different sizes.

questions.question: Find the order of rotational symmetry for a rhombus that is not a square.
questions.answer: The order of rotational symmetry for a rhombus is 2.

questions.question: In ∆PQR and ∆XYZ, PQ=XY, QR=YZ and ∠Q=∠Y. Name the congruence criterion.
questions.answer: SAS (Side-Angle-Side) congruence criterion.

questions.question: Name a quadrilateral that has both horizontal and vertical lines of symmetry but is not a regular polygon.
questions.answer: A Rectangle.

questions.question: What is the angle of rotation for a regular octagon?
If ∆LMN ≅ ∆XYZ and LM=5cm, ∠L=50°, what is the measure of XY and ∠X?
questions.answer: XY = 5cm and ∠X = 50°

questions.question: Explain why a scalene triangle has an order of rotational symmetry of 1.
questions.answer: It only looks the same after a full 360° turn.

questions.question: Under what condition is the RHS congruence criterion applied?
questions.answer: When two right-angled triangles have equal hypotenuses and one pair of equal sides.

questions.question: Draw a figure that has more than 3 lines of symmetry but no rotational symmetry (Note: Is this possible for a 2D plane figure?)
questions.answer: No, any plane figure with 'n' lines of symmetry must have rotational symmetry of at least order 'n'.

questions.question: In ∆ABC, if AB=AC and AD is the bisector of ∠A meeting BC at D, prove ∆ABD ≅ ∆ACD.
questions.answer: AB=AC (Given), ∠BAD=∠CAD (Bisector), AD=AD (Common). By SAS, ∆ABD ≅ ∆ACD.

questions.question: Which criterion is used if two angles and the included side of one triangle are equal to those of another?
questions.answer: ASA (Angle-Side-Angle) criterion.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry does a semi-circle have?
questions.answer: One line of symmetry (the perpendicular bisector of the diameter).

questions.question: If ∆DEF ≅ ∆BCA, write the part of ∆BCA that corresponds to ∠E.
questions.answer: ∠C

questions.question: What is the center of rotation for a rectangle?
questions.answer: The point of intersection of its diagonals.

questions.question: Can a triangle have two lines of symmetry?
questions.answer: No, if it has two, it must automatically have three (Equilateral).

questions.question: In an isosceles triangle, name the line of symmetry.
questions.answer: The median/altitude to the unequal side.

questions.question: State the SSS congruence criterion.
questions.answer: Two triangles are congruent if the three sides of one triangle are equal to the three sides of the other.

questions.question: A figure is rotated by 120° and it matches its original position. What is the order of rotation?
Is every square a regular polygon? How many lines of symmetry does it have?
questions.answer: Yes, it has 4 lines of symmetry.

questions.question: Are all congruent triangles similar? Are all similar triangles congruent?
questions.answer: Yes, all congruent triangles are similar. No, similar triangles are not necessarily congruent.

questions.question: If two squares have the same area, are they congruent?
questions.answer: Yes, because equal area implies equal side lengths ($Side = \sqrt{Area}$).

questions.question: Does a parallelogram (not a rhombus or rectangle) have any line of symmetry?
questions.answer: No, it has no line of symmetry but has rotational symmetry of order 2.

questions.question: Write the order and angle of rotational symmetry for a circle.
questions.answer: Order: Infinite; Angle: Any angle around the center.
